title: NOC 72310 – Carpenters | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Carpenters (NOC 72310) are the essential builders of Canada’s future, standing at the forefront of the national push to deliver over 5.8 million homes by 2030. Modern Canadian carpentry has evolved into a high-precision trade, utilizing sustainable mass timber, prefabricated modular components, and advanced digital measurement tools. Classified as TEER 2, this is a highly versatile trade where you can specialize in everything from rough framing and heavy formwork to delicate interior finishing and cabinet installation. With nearly 31,000 new job openings projected through 2033, carpenters remain one of the most in-demand professionals in the Canadian labor market.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Requires college, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group723 – Technical construction trades

duties: Blueprint Analysis: Reading and interpreting blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine specifications and calculate material requirements.
Layout & Measurement: Preparing layouts in conformance to building codes, using sophisticated measuring tools and laser levels.
Structural Assembly: Measuring, cutting, shaping, and joining materials made of wood, wood substitutes, and lightweight steel.
Installation: Fitting and installing trim items such as doors, stairs, moulding, and hardware to high-precision standards.
Foundation & Framing: Building foundations, installing floor beams, laying subflooring, and erecting wall and roof systems.
Maintenance & Repair: Renovating residences and wooden structures in mills, mines, hospitals, and industrial plants.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and some college courses is required for trade certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Quebec and voluntary in all other provinces/territories. However, certification is highly recommended for higher pay and union roles.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province without further testing.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for carpenters is Strong to Very High, driven by unprecedented residential and commercial construction targets:

Ontario: Very High Demand. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72310 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Minimum scores for this draw started at 50, making it one of the most accessible pathways for tradespeople with an Ontario job offer.

British Columbia: Critical Need. Carpenters are currently on the BC PNP Priority Construction Occupations List. Candidates with a valid trade certificate and a BC job offer can benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.
Alberta: Strong Demand. Carpenters in Alberta earn competitive wages, with a median of $32.00/hr and top earners in specialized industrial or high-end residential sectors reaching $45.00/hr.

National Trend: Approximately 126,000 people work in this occupation. The market is shifting toward "Green Building" specialists who can work with high-performance insulation and low-carbon wood products.
Why Work in Canada as a Carpenter?
Recession-Resistant Career: Your skills are the backbone of the "National Housing Strategy," ensuring steady work for the next decade.
Technological Integration: Lead the "Modular Revolution" by working with factory-built wall systems and Computer-Aided Design (CAD) layouts on-site.
Entrepreneurial Growth: Over 25% of carpenters in Canada are self-employed, providing a clear path to owning your own contracting business.
Immigration Priority: Carpenters are a core occupation in the 2026 Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category, frequently receiving Invitations to Apply (ITAs) at lower CRS thresholds.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Renewed for 2026. Note that the eligibility requirement has been increased to 12 months of full-time experience (or equivalent part-time) within the last 3 years.

OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72310 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.
BC PNP Construction Stream: Provides priority processing and targeted invitations for carpenters with a valid trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC and a full-time job offer.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Carpenters in the construction sector are classified as "High-Priority Essential Workers."
